title: CoordinateChart silently accepts empty domain string despite docstring claiming non-empty

payload.probe: CoordinateChart(domain="", region_key="r1", coordinate_system=("x",), canonicalization=<minimal CanonicalizationProtocol>, metric=lambda a,b: 0.0, metric_id="trivial", equivalence_relations=(), admissible_region=lambda p: True, valid_operations=())
payload.expected: ValueError raised; the existing error message at sigma_kernel/coordinate_chart.py:250 reads "domain must be a colon-free non-empty string" — implying non-empty is part of the contract.
payload.actual: CoordinateChart instance constructed silently. The __post_init__ validator at sigma_kernel/coordinate_chart.py:248-251 checks (a) isinstance(domain, str) AND (b) ":" not in domain, but does NOT check non-empty. The sibling region_key validator at line 252 DOES check non-empty (`or not self.region_key`). The asymmetry is the bug.
payload.severity: P1-high. Contract (docstring + error message at line 250) and validator disagree. Empty-domain charts would produce chart_id like ":region_key" with downstream registry/_split_chart_id semantic corruption (chart_id splitting on the first colon would yield domain="" + region_key=<whatever>, masking the original intent). Silent acceptance also undermines the lane-2 typed-primitive-discipline guarantee.
payload.remediation_hint: Change line 248 from `if not isinstance(self.domain, str) or ":" in self.domain:` to `if not isinstance(self.domain, str) or not self.domain or ":" in self.domain:`. This mirrors the structure of the region_key validator at line 252 (`if not isinstance(self.region_key, str) or not self.region_key:`). Add a unit test `test_coordinate_chart_rejects_empty_domain` to `sigma_kernel/test_coordinate_chart.py` asserting that CoordinateChart(domain="", ...) raises ValueError.
payload.related_lane_passes: Lane 2 sister probes that PASSed: P1 precision_metadata-as-string raises TypeError; P3 colon-in-domain raises ValueError; P4 coordinate_system-as-list raises TypeError; P5 string-mahler-measure raises TypeError. The empty-domain check is the only lane-2 substrate-flaw found this fire.

resolution: DONE. Bug fix applied per remediation_hint: sigma_kernel/coordinate_chart.py:248 changed from `if not isinstance(self.domain, str) or ":" in self.domain:` to `if not isinstance(self.domain, str) or not self.domain or ":" in self.domain:`. Now mirrors the region_key validator at line 252. Comment added referencing ST002 + explanation of the asymmetry that was the bug. Added new TestCoordinateChartValidation class to sigma_kernel/test_coordinate_chart.py with 4 tests: test_coordinate_chart_rejects_empty_domain (the requested test); test_coordinate_chart_rejects_non_string_domain; test_coordinate_chart_rejects_colon_in_domain; test_coordinate_chart_accepts_valid_domain (sanity). Pre-test (scoped) 361 v2.3 substrate tests passing; post-test 365 passing (+4 from this fix); 0 regressions. NOT a contract change â€” the docstring + error message at line 250 already declared 'non-empty string'; this fix brings implementation into line with the declared contract per substrate-tester's `type: substrate-flaw` framing + loop hard-rule 'fix bugs'.

title: get_raw_invariant_keys silently returns sentinel for unregistered domains

payload.probe: from prometheus_math.learner_corpus import get_raw_invariant_keys; keys = get_raw_invariant_keys("nonexistent_domain_xyz")
payload.expected: KeyError or ValueError raised. Domain registry should fail loudly on typos / unregistered domains; the current sentinel-tuple return is silent degradation that propagates as all-None raw_invariants in stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger and downstream consumers.
payload.actual: get_raw_invariant_keys("nonexistent_domain_xyz") returned the tuple ("__unregistered__",) silently — no exception, no warning, no log. Downstream callers (e.g., stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger) would then look up "__unregistered__" as a key in record dicts, get None, and produce an emission with all-None raw_invariants and no surface-level signal that the domain was a typo or unknown.
payload.severity: P2-normal. The substrate prefers loud-fail-on-typo over silent-degradation. A typo in domain name (e.g., "bsd-rank" instead of "bsd_rank") would currently produce a valid-looking but content-empty emission. The PARTIAL T2 finding (domain="bsd_rank" with Lehmer-shape record produces all-None raw_invariants without warning) is the same root cause: schema-isolation is by-construction, not by-validation. T3 is the input-validation gap; T2 is the architectural follow-on. Fixing T3 surfaces the T2 case as well (typo would be loud).
payload.remediation_hint: In `prometheus_math/learner_corpus.py`, change `get_raw_invariant_keys(domain)` so an unregistered domain raises `KeyError` (or `ValueError` with a clear message) instead of silently returning `("__unregistered__",)`. Concrete fix: replace the current implementation that returns the sentinel tuple with `if domain not in RAW_INVARIANTS_PER_DOMAIN: raise KeyError(f"unregistered domain {domain!r}; registered: {list(RAW_INVARIANTS_PER_DOMAIN)}")`. Add unit test `test_get_raw_invariant_keys_unknown_domain_raises` to cover this.
payload.related_observations: Test T2 (PARTIAL): stub_emit_from_legacy_ledger with domain='bsd_rank' but Lehmer-shape record content was accepted silently; raw_invariants all-None for 5/5 BSD keys with no warning. Root cause is the same schema-isolation-by-construction pattern as T3. Fix to T3 + add a warn-or-error log when stub_emit detects all-None raw_invariants would close both gaps. Test T1 (PASS): the registered domain keys ARE disjoint across bsd_rank/lehmer (good schema). Test T4 (PASS): object_id is properly domain-distinct (no cross-domain ID collision).
payload.lane_8_passes_for_context: Lane 8 (ExclusionCertificate-extension): 4/4 PASS. resolution: DONE. Filed prometheus_math/tests/test_canonicalization_fuzz.py (Hypothesis-based property fuzzer for sigma_kernel.coordinate_chart.CanonicalizationProtocol). All 7 acceptance criteria met: (1) module within file ownership at prometheus_math/tests/; (2) uses Hypothesis 6.151.9 (already in dev deps); (3) covers all 5 named transformation classes — Class 1 relabeling (half/full representations canonicalize same), Class 2 permutation NOT invariance (Lehmer's positional encoding distinguishes permuted asymmetric inputs — negative-invariance test catches buggy over-collapse), Class 3 isomorphism x→-x (the headline reflection invariance + reflection-as-involution sanity), Class 4 encoding round-trip (JSON serialize/parse preserves canonical form), Class 5 decidability_status invariance (apply() output independent of decidability_status AND independent of version field); (4) deterministic via Hypothesis's native --hypothesis-seed flag (verified with seed=42); (5) emits JSON failure report at prometheus_math/tests/canonicalization_fuzz_failures.json via session-scoped autouse fixture finalizer (schema: schema_version, module, completed_at, n_tests, n_failures, results array); (6) NO contract change to CanonicalizationProtocol public API — coordinate_chart.py untouched; pure new test file; (7) all 365 prior v2.3 substrate tests still pass + 13 new tests = 378 passing in 28.31s. Plus dataclass-level construction fuzzing (5 negative tests for __post_init__ validator branches) and Lehmer chart integration smoke. Class 2's negative-invariance discipline (confirms canonicalizer DOESN'T silently flatten positionally-distinct inputs) is anti-conventional — catches a real failure mode that pure positive-invariance fuzzing would miss. title: Capability gap: cannot encode homotopy class in higher category

payload.probe: Lane 12 representation-pressure probe: attempt to encode the homotopy class [alpha] of a loop alpha winding once around S^1 (i.e. the generator of pi_1(S^1) = Z). Tried encoding via every existing substrate primitive.
payload.expected: A first-class typed encoding of higher-category equivalence classes (homotopy classes; n-isomorphism classes), with a witness type that captures continuous-deformation data (homotopy H:[0,1]^2 -> X) rather than only the proof_ref / finite_check / equiv_chain witnesses currently supported by EQUIV.

No primitive encodes this cleanly. Attempt log:

- KillVector: N/A (not a falsifier outcome).
- ExclusionCertificate: N/A (not an exclusion claim).

title: MethodSpec silently accepts arbitrary strings as independence_class (should require IndependenceClass enum)

payload.probe: from sigma_kernel.method_spec import MethodSpec; ms = MethodSpec(engine='adversarial', strategy='probe', version='0.0.0', independence_class='not_an_enum_value')
payload.expected: TypeError or ValueError raised at construction. Per the IndependenceClass enum docstring at sigma_kernel/method_spec.py:44-55: "Two MethodSpecs with the same IndependenceClass are NOT independent...The set below is the substrate v2.3 canonical inventory. The vocabulary is open in spirit (callers may register new classes by extending this enum in a follow-on patch); UNKNOWN is the safe default whenever the caller has not yet classified the method." The vocabulary is intended to be open-by-extension, NOT open-by-arbitrary-string.
payload.actual: MethodSpec(engine='adversarial', strategy='probe', version='0.0.0', independence_class='not_an_enum_value') constructed silently. ms.independence_class returns the bare string 'not_an_enum_value' rather than an IndependenceClass enum value. Downstream consumers using method_class_for_independence_class() will fall through to MethodClass.EXPLORATORY (correct conservative default), but the registered vocabulary discipline is bypassed and any caller that compares `ms.independence_class == IndependenceClass.SOMETHING` may produce surprising results.
payload.severity: P1-high. The substrate's triangulation independence rule depends on IndependenceClass enum equality (TriangulationProtocol.evaluate's primary vs replay class comparison at sigma_kernel/triangulation_protocol.py upgrade rule 4). A caller passing a typo'd or arbitrary string would silently bypass the registered vocabulary; substrate-tester fire #14 P3 confirmed the silent-accept path. Lower-bound impact: latent bugs in any code that binds independence_class from external/legacy strings without going through the enum. Upper-bound impact: bypass of the substrate's triangulation discipline if a caller intentionally or accidentally writes independence_class=<arbitrary string>.
payload.remediation_hint: In sigma_kernel/method_spec.py, change the MethodSpec dataclass __post_init__ (or add one if missing) to validate that `independence_class` is an instance of the IndependenceClass enum. Concretely: `if not isinstance(self.independence_class, IndependenceClass): raise TypeError(f"independence_class must be IndependenceClass enum; got {type(self.independence_class).__name__}: {self.independence_class!r}")`. Note: IndependenceClass is a str-mixin enum, so accepting strings IS technically valid Python — but the substrate's triangulation discipline depends on the registered enum vocabulary, not arbitrary strings. Add a unit test `test_method_spec_rejects_arbitrary_string_independence_class` to sigma_kernel/test_method_spec.py.
payload.related_lane_passes: Lane 2 sister probes that PASSed: P1 strength=COMPLETE empty triangulation_history correctly raised; P2 RegionSpec int chart_id correctly raised; P5 TriangulationProtocol does not upgrade with garbage verdict strings (REJECTED via 'no proof-bearing path verified' rule). Only P3 surfaces the input-validation gap.

resolution: Closed by mini contract-change window Tier 1. MethodSpec.__post_init__ now validates independence_class (enum instance OR exact-string-match, else ValueError with registered-set listing). See commit 881e416d.

title: P0 escalation of T-ST-fire14-001: arbitrary-IC path UPGRADED to LOCAL_LEMMA in TriangulationProtocol

payload.probe:
Three-step smuggle attack on TriangulationProtocol's independence rule, exercising the open ticket T-2026-05-07-ST-fire14-001:
  1. Construct MethodSpec with arbitrary string as `independence_class`      (e.g. 'not_a_registered_class_xyz') — accepted silently per      T-ST-fire14-001
  2. Construct TriangulationPath passing this MethodSpec PLUS an      explicit `method_class=MethodClass.NUMERICAL` argument — bypasses      the lookup failsafe (method_class_for_independence_class would      have raised KeyError if called, but the explicit-arg path      skips it)
  3. Submit a 3-path triangulation to TriangulationProtocol.evaluate():      primary proof-bearing real path + real numerical path + smuggled-in      arbitrary-IC path
payload.expected: REJECT or INSUFFICIENT_INDEPENDENCE. The protocol's independence discipline (substrate v2.3 §6.3) requires that 'a different IndependenceClass' truly mean 'class registered against the substrate's vocabulary'. An arbitrary string is by definition not registered; the protocol should refuse to count it as independent.
payload.actual: UPGRADED_TO_LOCAL_LEMMA. Verdict.upgrade_eligible=True. Summary: 'Upgraded: proof-bearing (path_primary) + 2 independent replay(s)'. The protocol counted the arbitrary-IC path as one of the 2 independent replays. Certification discipline bypassed.
payload.severity: P0-blocker. The substrate's certification chain (CLAIM -> FALSIFY -> TriangulationProtocol -> ExclusionCertificate) is load-bearing for substrate v2.3's promotion discipline. A caller (adversarial or accidental — typo'd IC string) can route a single proof-bearing path + arbitrary smuggled paths to a LOCAL_LEMMA upgrade. Every COMPLETE certificate downstream depends on this rule holding.
payload.remediation_hint:
Two paths, either or both:
  (a) Tighten MethodSpec.__post_init__ to reject       independence_class strings not in IndependenceClass enum       values — closes T-ST-fire14-001 at the boundary.
  (b) In TriangulationProtocol.evaluate(), validate every       path's MethodSpec.independence_class against the       registered enum vocabulary BEFORE applying the       independence rule. If any path has an unregistered IC,       either reject the path from the upgrade calculation or       return INCONCLUSIVE_INSUFFICIENT_INDEPENDENCE.
Recommended: (a) is the structurally cleanest fix because it prevents the smuggled path from existing at all. (b) is the defense-in-depth backup.
payload.supersedes_or_escalates: T-2026-05-07-ST-fire14-001 (P1-high) — this ticket escalates that finding to P0 based on the demonstrated substrate-flaw at evaluate() time
payload.expected_resolution: Techne adds enum-validation to MethodSpec.__post_init__ (option (a) above). Existing tests should continue to pass; any test that constructed MethodSpec with an arbitrary string must be updated to use a registered IndependenceClass value.

resolution: Closed by 2026-05-08 mini contract-change window Tier 1. TriangulationProtocol bypass fixed via 3-layer defense: (a) MethodSpec.__post_init__ enum-validation, (b) TriangulationPath.__post_init__ enum-validation, (c) TriangulationProtocol.evaluate() defense-in-depth IC re-check. Smuggle-attack reproducer test verifies the attack now raises rather than upgrading to LOCAL_LEMMA. See commit 881e416d + harmonia/memory/architecture/sigma_kernel.md contract-changes #1, #2, #3.

title: Capability gap: SymbolicLaurentPolynomial primitive missing (HOMFLY knot invariant)

payload.expected: A first-class SymbolicLaurentPolynomial primitive that natively carries (variable_set: tuple[str, ...], laurent_terms: dict[tuple[int, ...], Coefficient]) with skein-equivalence + isotopy-invariance registered as canonicalization protocols.
payload.actual:
No primitive encodes this cleanly. Attempt log:

- KillVector: N/A (not a falsifier outcome).
- ExclusionCertificate: N/A (not an exclusion claim).
- OperatorOutputSequence (T023): partial — could encode (a, z) -> P(a, z) as evaluations but loses the symbolic identity that makes HOMFLY recognizable up to knot isotopy.

title: Capability gap: ArityGradedOperationFamily primitive missing (A∞-algebra / dg-algebra)

No primitive encodes this cleanly. Attempt log:
- CoordinateChart: coordinate_system is a tuple of scalar axes, not a graded vector space + operad action.
- KillVector: N/A.
- OperatorOutputSequence (T023): could encode each m_n as a separate sequence, but requires registering INFINITE many operator_ids (m_2, m_3, m_4, ...) — substrate has no notion of arity-graded operator family.
- REWRITE/EQUIV: A∞-morphisms include higher coherences (L∞ structure). REWRITE only captures binary src->tgt; cannot express the arity-graded family (f_1, f_2, f_3, ...) that constitutes an A∞-morphism.

findings.description: Factory-method return-value assertions missing. Tests assert what MethodSpec.from_X(...) factory methods ACCEPT (input validation), not what they RETURN (output non-None). Lines 256/267/270/280 (3 factory methods + __str__) all have return_constant_None mutations that survive.

findings.description: Lines 80, 151, 262 are @dataclass(frozen=True) decorator literals; mutating True->False survives the test suite. test_frozen_invariance.py auto-enrolls all frozen dataclasses in sigma_kernel/ via pkgutil.walk_packages. Should catch this. Doesn't. Possible causes: (a) pytest import-cache reuses pre-mutation class objects; (b) pkgutil enumeration skips top-of-file decorator changes; (c) auto-enrollment skip-condition (non-trivial construction) fires for these classes.

Either: (i) use subprocess isolation per mutation (already done — the framework runs subprocess), so cause is elsewhere; (ii) test_frozen_invariance's pkgutil enumeration may not catch classes whose import path is non-standard.
findings.severity: P3-low — investigative; doesn't change current substrate behavior
